content_en: They all recorded this hadith through the chain of Dawood ibn Abu Hind on the authority of Makhool from Abu Thalaba. The narrators in these chains are, in general, of sahih quality. The problem, however, with this chain is the link between Makhool and Abu Thalaba. The majority of the scholars, including Abu Zura and Abu Haatim, state that Makhool did not hear hadith from Abu Thalaba.1 But, as al­ Haitami points out, ibn Maeen disagreed with them. He said that Makhool did hear hadith from Abu Thalaba. 2 The general rule in a case like this is that the claim of affirmation takes precedence over the negation. This is because the scholar who affirms the narration must have access to some knowledge that affirms that the one scholar heard from the other. The information that he is relying upon was probably unknown to the other scholars. This is why they deny the connection between the two. This, though, does not solve the problem with the chain at that point. If Makhool did hear from Abu Thalaba, there is a new problem. This problem is that Makhool was known for committing tadlees.3 He has been classified by ibn Hajr and others as being from the third category of those who commit tadlees.4 This means that his hadith will not be accepted on their own unless he uses an unequivocal term showing that he heard the hadith directly from the 1 See ibn Rajab, Jaami, vol. 2, p. 1 50. Ibn Rajab points out a second defect with this hadith. Sometimes it is narrated as a statement of Abu Thalaba rather than a hadith of the Prophet (peace be upon him). However, that problem is inconsequential since the scholars of hadith have determined that the correct narration is that going back to the Prophet (peace be upon him) and not stopping at Abu Thalaba. 2 Al-Haitami, Fath, p. 230. 3 Tadlees is where a person narrates a hadith from one of his teachers but the particular hadith he is narrating he did not hear directly from his teacher but through an intermediate source. The narrator uses the ambiguous word an (UC, "on the authority of'). This term is ambiguous because, lexically, it does not deny or confirm that he heard it directly from his teacher. Technically speaking, the person is not lying but he is doing something that is very bad in the eyes of the scholars of hadith, since it makes it unclear if the chain is missing any narrators or not. If a person is accused of committing tadlees, then, in general, his hadith will not be accepted unless he uses a term that explicitly demonstrates that he heard the hadith directly from his teacher and not possibly from an intermediate source. 4 Cf., Misfur al-Dumaini, al-Tadlees fl al-hadith (Published by its author, 1 992), pp. 352-355. Hadith #30: "Allah has obligated the Obligatory Deeds . . . " source he stated. In this particular chain, he used the word an ("on the authority of'). I I I I Nahshal alI Khurasaani I I Al-Daraqutni The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) I Abu alI Darda Taawoos I al-Dhuhaak I ibn Muzaahim I Qurrah ibn Khalid Asram ibn Haushab al-Tabaraani, ibn Adi I I I Abu Thalaba I I I I Makhool I Dawud ibn l Abu Hind ' Al-Oaraqutni, al-Baihaqi, al-Haakim, ibn Battah, Abu Nuaim and others Figure 30.1 The chains for Hadith Number 30 Key: Heavy black connecting line indicates problem at that link; heavy box indicates problem with that narrator; dotted line with arrow means narrators intentionally left out for presentation purposes only Therefore, either this chain is broken between Makhool and Abu Thalaba or, if that is not the case, there is a problem with Makhool's tadlees Commentary on the Forty Hadith of al-Nawawi and his use of the word an here. 1 Hence, this particular chain is not acceptable on its own. It is in need of corroborating evidence to be considered an acceptable hadith. There is another possible problem with this chain that seems to have gone unnoticed by those who commented on this hadith. The hadith is narrated through Dawood ibn Abu Hind. There is no doubt that he is a trustworthy narrator. Ibn Hibban noted that he used to make a few mistakes but not enough to reject his hadith. However, according to ibn Hajr and al-Dhahabi, toward the end of his life he became somewhat senile or lost some of his proficiency and began to make mistakes in his narration of hadith.2 Dawood died in the year 139 or 140 A.H. and it is said that he was 75 years old.3 In Sunan al-Daaraqutni, for example, the narrator who narrated this hadith from Dawood was Ishaaq al-Azraq. Ishaaq was born in the year 1 17.4 Assuming he started recording hadith at the young age of 15, Dawood must have been at least in his late sixties before Ishaaq could have studied and heard hadith from him.
content_id:
Semua perawi ini merekam hadis ini melalui sanad Dawud ibn Abi Hind dari Makhul dari Abu Tsa'labah. Para perawi dalam sanad-sanad ini, secara umum, berkualitas sahih. Namun, masalah dengan sanad ini adalah hubungan antara Makhul dan Abu Tsa'labah. Mayoritas ulama, termasuk Abu Zur'ah dan Abu Hatim, menyatakan bahwa Makhul tidak mendengar hadis dari Abu Tsa'labah. Namun, sebagaimana Al-Haitami tunjukkan, Ibn Ma'in tidak setuju dengan mereka. Beliau mengatakan bahwa Makhul memang mendengar hadis dari Abu Tsa'labah.

Aturan umum dalam kasus seperti ini adalah bahwa klaim penegasan lebih didahulukan daripada penafian. Ini karena ulama yang menegaskan periwayatan pasti memiliki akses pada pengetahuan tertentu yang menegaskan bahwa satu ulama mendengar dari yang lain. Informasi yang beliau andalkan mungkin tidak diketahui oleh ulama-ulama lain. Inilah mengapa mereka menyangkal hubungan antara keduanya. Namun, ini tidak menyelesaikan masalah dengan sanad pada titik itu. Jika Makhul memang mendengar dari Abu Tsa'labah, ada masalah baru. Masalah ini adalah bahwa Makhul dikenal melakukan tadlis. Beliau telah diklasifikasikan oleh Ibn Hajar dan lainnya sebagai termasuk dalam kategori ketiga dari mereka yang melakukan tadlis. Ini berarti hadis-hadisnya tidak akan diterima dengan sendirinya kecuali beliau menggunakan istilah yang jelas dan tegas yang menunjukkan bahwa beliau mendengar hadis langsung dari sumbernya.

Tadlis adalah ketika seseorang meriwayatkan hadis dari salah satu gurunya tetapi hadis tertentu yang beliau riwayatkan tidak beliau dengar langsung dari gurunya melainkan melalui sumber perantara. Perawi menggunakan kata yang ambigu 'an' ('dari'). Istilah ini ambigu karena secara leksikal tidak menyangkal atau mengkonfirmasi bahwa beliau mendengarnya langsung dari gurunya. Secara teknis, orang tersebut tidak berbohong tetapi beliau melakukan sesuatu yang sangat buruk di mata para ulama hadis, karena hal itu membuat tidak jelas apakah sanad tersebut kehilangan perawi atau tidak. Jika seseorang dituduh melakukan tadlis, maka secara umum hadis-hadisnya tidak akan diterima kecuali beliau menggunakan istilah yang secara eksplisit menunjukkan bahwa beliau mendengar hadis langsung dari gurunya. Dalam sanad khusus ini, beliau menggunakan kata 'an.'

Oleh karena itu, sanad ini tidak dapat diterima dengan sendirinya. Sanad ini memerlukan bukti pendukung untuk dianggap sebagai hadis yang dapat diterima. Ada kemungkinan masalah lain dengan sanad ini yang tampaknya luput dari perhatian mereka yang mengomentari hadis ini. Hadis ini diriwayatkan melalui Dawud ibn Abi Hind. Tidak diragukan lagi bahwa beliau adalah perawi yang terpercaya. Ibn Hibban mencatat bahwa beliau biasa membuat beberapa kesalahan tetapi tidak cukup untuk menolak hadis-hadisnya. Namun, menurut Ibn Hajar dan Al-Dzahabi, menjelang akhir hidupnya beliau menjadi agak pikun atau kehilangan sebagian kemampuannya dan mulai membuat kesalahan dalam periwayatan hadis. Dawud wafat pada tahun 139 atau 140 H dan dikatakan berusia 75 tahun. Dalam Sunan Ad-Daraquthni misalnya, perawi yang meriwayatkan hadis ini dari Dawud adalah Ishaq Al-Azraq. Ishaq lahir pada tahun 117 H. Dengan asumsi beliau mulai mencatat hadis pada usia muda 15 tahun, Dawud paling tidak harus sudah berusia akhir enam puluhan sebelum Ishaq dapat belajar dan mendengar hadis darinya.
